input.background: A random-digit dialing technique is used to select 57 individuals from a community.
input.question: Seventeen of 57 individuals in the sample are classified as smokers.

output.answer: Therefore, the proportion of smokers in the sample \( \hat{p}=\frac{x}{n}=\frac{17}{57}=0.298 \). Assuming the sample is an SRS and the information is properly classified, this is an unbiased estimate of the prevalence of smoking in the population.

total_individuals.description: The total number of individuals selected from the community.

smokers.description: The number of individuals in the sample classified as smokers.

non_smokers.description: The number of individuals in the sample classified as non-smokers, calculated as the difference between total individuals and smokers.

meta_info.caption: Data (Prevalence of smoking in a community).
